_note: SYNTHETIC DEMO FIXTURE — not investment advice. Pre-call structured input for the Diligence-Call Copilot demo, grounded in the topics Apple raised on its Q2 FY2026 earnings call (Apr 30 2026): the fund's question list + thesis + modeled assumptions the copilot grounds follow-ups and contradiction flags against.
thesis: AAPL enters the print mid-supercycle: the iPhone 17 family is driving 20%+ revenue growth — the strongest cycle in company history — while Mac (MacBook Neo, plus mini/Studio as local-AI machines) runs ahead of supply. Growth is gated by supply, not demand: the binding constraint is advanced-node SOC availability (iPhone in the March quarter, shifting to Mac into June). The central risk is the memory-cost overhang on gross margin, modest now but building beyond June. Services keeps compounding at high margin with advertising (App Store, now Maps) a fresh lever; capital allocation is shifting as Apple drops the net-cash-neutral target and lifts buyback authorization by $100B; AI pairs a Google partnership with in-house foundational models on accelerating R&D.
thesis_memo: We are long AAPL into a genuine, supply-gated hardware supercycle. Two load-bearing checks for this call: (1) is the growth real demand outrunning supply, with the constraint correctly diagnosed (advanced-node SOC availability, iPhone->Mac into June) rather than a demand pull-forward; and (2) does the gross-margin story survive the memory-cost overhang building beyond June, alongside the capital-allocation shift (dropping net-cash-neutral, +$100B buyback). If management glosses the memory trajectory or the bridge, the margin leg is weaker than the headline suggests.

modeled_assumptions.iphone_cycle: iPhone revenue +~22%, strongest cycle in company history; iPhone 17 family; March-quarter upgrader record
modeled_assumptions.supply_constraint: supply-gated, not demand-gated; iPhone binding in March quarter, shifting to Mac (mini, Studio, MacBook Neo) into June; root cause advanced-node SOC availability
modeled_assumptions.gross_margin: company GM ~49%; memory minimal-to-modest now, building headwind beyond June; mix + lower tariffs help, seasonal deleverage hurts, FX ~neutral
modeled_assumptions.capital_allocation: net-cash-neutral target dropped; cash and debt evaluated independently; buyback authorization +$100B
modeled_assumptions.services_ads_ai: Services compounding at high margin (services GM +~20bps QoQ on mix); App Store search ads + Apple Maps ads launching US/Canada this summer; AI = Google partnership + in-house foundational models, R&D accelerating faster than opex

pillars.thesis: Supply-gated hardware supercycle: demand is outrunning supply across iPhone and Mac
Margin & capital: a memory-cost overhang against a shifting capital posture
The forward call: Services and ads, plus the AI strategy

questions.text: iPhone 17 — where are you supply-constrained, iPhone versus Mac, and what's the root cause?
questions.why: The cycle is the spine of the thesis. We need the growth confirmed as supply-gated demand (not a pull-forward) and the constraint correctly diagnosed (advanced-node SOC, iPhone->Mac into June).
questions.demo_role: clean tick — answered early and cleanly
notes: iPhone +22% YoY; constraint is SOC, not memory — Q2 FY26 call
iPhone-led → Mac in June; China 1H +33% — Q2 FY26 call
Supply-gated, not a pull-forward — Vantage note

questions.text: Where's gross margin, and how do I think about it from here given memory costs and the capital-allocation shift?
questions.why: Margin durability is the load-bearing check. The note models a memory-cost headwind building beyond June and a capital-allocation shift; if management glosses the bridge, the margin leg is weaker than the headline.
questions.demo_role: HERO — researcher over-claims 'margins are great, nothing to flag' -> partial/amber + contradiction vs note -> copilot fires grounded follow-up -> closes green with the real bridge + capital shift
notes: Memory rising into June and beyond — Q2 FY26 call

questions.text: How durable are Services and the ad lever, and what's the AI strategy costing you?
questions.why: Services and advertising are the high-margin compounder; the AI strategy (in-house vs Google) and its R&D cost frame the multiple.
notes: Services GM +20bps; ads up YoY — Q2 FY26 call
Maps ads launch US/Canada this summer — Q2 FY26 call
Google + in-house models; R&D rising — Q2 FY26 call
